# Transcrição: 02- Accessing Client Ad Accounts 

- **Fonte:** 02- Accessing Client Ad Accounts 
- **Duração:** 04:16
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:01]

Hey guys, so in this video we're going to go over how to request access to a client ad account or Facebook page. So if you're just doing an audit you just need analyst access and you don't need access to the Facebook page. However, if you're doing more than that and you're looking to start advertising, you're going to need access to both the ad account as well as the Facebook page. So make sure you explain this to the client as there may be some confusion there. They're going to be getting two different requests, two separate requests saying, hey, we need access to your Facebook page as well as we need access to your ad account. So just make sure that's something that you communicate to your clients prior to sending those off. So if you're looking to add a Facebook page, you just click right under here under accounts, you go to pages, click pages, and then you click add. So you want to make sure here that you don't click add a page. This means you're going to claim it to your business manager, which is something you don't want to do. You want to make sure that the client has this asset in their business manager. So what you want to do is request access to a page. So here you can either type in the page name or you can directly transfer the URL right in here. So something to note as well, if the page name has changed recently, you want to make sure that you insert the URL here. Otherwise, you might not be able to find that page. So let's say I want to claim a loop social. So that would be there. And then here is where you request what you want from the page. So here I don't need earnings insights. I don't need to manage jobs. If you're just doing an audit or if you just if you just need analyst access, you would just need view page performance. If you're running ads for the page, you want to make sure you have create ads. You might want to moderate comments as well. And then you might also want to publish content. You're never going to need admin access. So that's never something that you really need to request. So moving on here, it's a similar idea with the ad accounts. You just need to click on the ad accounts tab here and then go click add again. And again, here it's not allowing me to add an ad account because this business manager has reached its capacity for ad accounts. However, this might show up on your ad account. So you want to make sure that you're just requesting access to an ad account instead of adding an ad account into the business manager, specifically if it's a client account. So it's a similar process here. You just click on request access. You type in the ad account ID. So let's just say this one. And then you just click exactly what you need. Again, if you just need analyst access, you just need to click view performance. If you want to actually manage and advertise for those campaigns, you need to click manage campaigns and then you click confirm. And then that'll send a notification to the client. Now, another way you can do this is by having your client add you in as a partner. So if you want them to add you in as a partner here, you just go to the partners tab and then you click add. And then you say you can either give a partner access to your assets or you can say ask a partner to share their assets. So this is what you want to do simply because you're asking them for access to their assets.

## [03:01 - 04:16]

So you click on here. Get started. This is a relatively new feature here. So then you add the contact name, the contact email. So let's say contact name is Derek. Contact email is Derek at eight loop. Social.com. And then you enter their business ID. So here's the business ID here. Contact info that'll be shared. So this is what this person will be receiving. This is my other business manager ID. So now here, what is your business's role? So you're more than likely a media agency here. So and then the next step here is the partner's role. So what is your partner in this? The partner would be your client in this case. So they are more than likely a brand or business. So then you just click next. And here is where you can select exactly what you want access to. So you can say, I want access to view the page performance to create ads, to moderate comments. Again, it's a very similar process to the other. Essentially what you do here and you can actually request access to everything in one. So you can request access to the ad accounts, the catalogs, the pixels, the Instagram accounts and the pages. So it's really, really easy. It makes it a lot less tedious. And it's something that Facebook has just recently released.
